title: 顺水推舟
summary:
In the early spring of 1946, Linnie "Lyn" Marchmont returns to White House, a Georgian-style home in the decaying village of Wormsley Valley, after completing her service in the Women's Royal Naval Service. While she finds comfort in the familiar surroundings and the absence of military routine, she experiences a growing sense of restlessness and dissatisfaction. Lyn observes that her family's financial situation has declined due to post-war taxes and rising costs, making her realize that their previous stability was entirely dependent on her wealthy, childless uncle, Gordon Claude.

During breakfast with her mother, Adela Marchmont, the conversation turns to the recent death of Gordon Claude in a foreign land. Lyn reflects on how Gordon had acted as a provider and protector for the entire family, including her cousin Rolly Claude. Rolly and his friend Johnny Vavasu had started a farming partnership with the implicit understanding that Gordon would provide financial support if they proved their capability. Lyn’s own future path seems set by the expectation that she will marry Rolly, a plan she has desired for years.

The family's sense of security was recently disrupted by Gordon’s unexpected marriage to a woman he met while traveling from South America to New York. Adela expresses surprise at the union, noting that Gordon had always seemed content with his familial ties. As Lyn contemplates the implications of her uncle's death and his sudden marriage, she recognizes that the financial cushion that once guaranteed their future has been compromised.
